title: Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Guide | Captain Tommy Moore
metaDescription: Book a Smith Mountain Lake fishing guide trip with Captain Tommy Moore. Expert SML striper guide service in Moneta, VA. Rods, reels, bait & tackle included.
content:
Your Expert Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Guide

When it comes to finding a reliable Smith Mountain Lake fishing guide, Captain Tommy Moore and SML Wicked Striper Guide Service stand out as the top choice for anglers of every skill level. Based in Moneta, VA 24121, Captain Tommy has spent years mastering the waters of Smith Mountain Lake, learning every cove, point, and underwater structure where trophy striped bass hold throughout the year.

Smith Mountain Lake, nestled in the Blue Ridge Mountains of Virginia, is one of the premier striped bass fisheries on the East Coast. Spanning over 20,000 acres with 500 miles of shoreline, this massive reservoir offers incredible opportunities for anglers — but only if you know where to look. That's where an experienced SML striper guide makes all the difference.

Why Captain Tommy Moore Is the Guide You Want

Captain Tommy doesn't just take you fishing — he puts you on fish. His deep knowledge of Smith Mountain Lake's seasonal patterns, bait migrations, and water conditions means you spend less time searching and more time with a bent rod. Whether you're chasing a personal best striper or introducing your kids to the sport, Captain Tommy tailors every trip to your goals.

Every guided trip includes:

  • Professional-grade rods, reels, and terminal tackle
  • Fresh live bait (gizzard shad and herring)
  • Coast Guard-approved personal flotation devices
  • Fish-finding electronics and GPS mapping
  • Decades of on-the-water experience at Smith Mountain Lake

What to Expect on a Guided Fishing Trip

A typical day with SML Wicked Striper Guide Service starts early. Captain Tommy meets you at the dock, gets you set up with gear, and briefs you on the day's game plan. Using his Humminbird electronics and years of pattern knowledge, he'll position the boat over actively feeding fish.

Most trips target striped bass using live bait presentations — downlining, free-lining, or planer board techniques depending on conditions. Captain Tommy adjusts tactics throughout the day based on what the fish are telling him. If the stripers are suspended at 35 feet over a main-lake hump, that's where you'll be. If they're chasing bait in a creek arm, you'll be right in the action.

Smith Mountain Lake: A World-Class Striper Fishery

Virginia's Department of Wildlife Resources stocks Smith Mountain Lake annually with striped bass fingerlings, maintaining a healthy and robust population. The lake regularly produces fish in the 20- to 40-pound range, with the occasional 50-pounder making headlines. The combination of deep, clear water and abundant forage (gizzard shad, blueback herring, and alewife) creates ideal conditions for growing massive stripers.

The lake's location in the Virginia Blue Ridge also means stunning scenery. Mountain vistas, bald eagles overhead, and peaceful coves make every trip memorable — even on the rare slow fishing day.

Booking Your Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Trip

Captain Tommy Moore offers half-day and full-day guided fishing trips year-round on Smith Mountain Lake. Trips depart from Moneta, VA, and are perfect for individuals, families, corporate groups, and tournament practice.

Ready to experience the best striper fishing Virginia has to offer? Book your trip with Captain Tommy Moore today or call (434) 610-3255 to check availability. Check our pricing page for current rates and trip options.

title: Best Time to Fish Smith Mountain Lake | Striper Seasons
metaDescription: Discover the best time to fish Smith Mountain Lake for stripers. Seasonal fishing guide covering spring, summer, fall & winter striper patterns in Virginia.
content:
Best Time to Fish Smith Mountain Lake for Striped Bass

One of the most common questions Captain Tommy Moore gets is: "What's the best time to fish Smith Mountain Lake?" The honest answer is that Smith Mountain Lake offers excellent striper fishing year-round — but understanding the striper fishing seasons helps you pick the ideal window for your trip.

Located in Virginia's Blue Ridge Mountains near Moneta, VA, Smith Mountain Lake benefits from a temperate climate that keeps striped bass active in every season. Here's your complete seasonal breakdown from SML Wicked Striper Guide Service.

Spring Striper Fishing (March – May)

Spring is arguably the most exciting time to fish Smith Mountain Lake. As water temperatures climb through the 50s and into the 60s, stripers move from deep winter haunts toward the upper reaches of the lake and tributary arms. This is the pre-spawn and spawn period, and fish are aggressive, hungry, and often found in shallower water than any other time of year.

Key spring patterns include:

  • Stripers chasing shad in creek channels and flats
  • Surface feeding activity during low-light periods
  • Fish staging on main-lake points before moving up tributaries
  • Excellent action on both live bait and artificial lures

Captain Tommy's spring trips frequently produce multiple fish over 20 pounds, with shots at true trophies in the 30- to 40-pound class.

Summer Striper Fishing (June – August)

Summer on Smith Mountain Lake means warm surface temperatures, a well-established thermocline, and stripers that settle into predictable deep-water patterns. Fish stack up in the thermocline zone, typically 25 to 45 feet deep, where water temperatures and dissolved oxygen levels are ideal.

This is prime time for downlining live bait — Captain Tommy's bread and butter technique. Using fresh gizzard shad presented at precise depths, you'll target schools of stripers holding over main-lake humps, channel bends, and deep points. Summer also brings reliable early-morning topwater blitzes that will get your heart pounding.

Fall Striper Fishing (September – November)

Fall is a sleeper season that many anglers overlook. As the lake turns over and water temperatures equalize, stripers go on a feeding binge to pack on weight for winter. Bait schools get pushed into coves and pockets, and hungry stripers follow them in.

Fall fishing on Smith Mountain Lake often produces the highest catch numbers of the year. Fish are scattered across the water column, which means multiple techniques come into play — from trolling umbrella rigs to casting topwater plugs to vertical jigging. Captain Tommy adjusts daily based on conditions.

Winter Striper Fishing (December – February)

Don't put the rods away when it gets cold. Winter striper fishing at Smith Mountain Lake can be phenomenal. Cold water concentrates fish into tight schools near deep structure, and when you find them, the action can be fast. Winter fish are also some of the fattest of the year, heavy with eggs and stored energy.

Captain Tommy uses his Humminbird electronics to locate deep schools, then presents live bait right in their wheelhouse. Water clarity is typically excellent in winter, and there's almost zero boat traffic — it's just you, the fish, and the mountains.

Book the Right Season for Your Goals

Want trophy-class fish? Book spring or winter. Want nonstop action? Fall is your season. Want a beautiful day on the water with consistent catching? Summer delivers every time. No matter when you come, Captain Tommy Moore will put you in the best position to succeed.

Book your Smith Mountain Lake fishing trip today or call Captain Tommy at (434) 610-3255 to discuss the best dates for your goals.

title: Why Hire a Fishing Guide at Smith Mountain Lake, VA
metaDescription: Wondering why hire a fishing guide? Learn how guided fishing trips in Virginia at Smith Mountain Lake save time, catch more fish & create lasting memories.
content:
Why Hire a Fishing Guide at Smith Mountain Lake?

If you've ever wondered why hire a fishing guide when you could just head out on your own, you're not alone. It's a fair question. But after one trip with Captain Tommy Moore on Smith Mountain Lake, you'll understand exactly why guided fishing trips in Virginia are worth every penny — especially on a 20,000-acre lake where stripers can be anywhere.

Here's the straight truth from SML Wicked Striper Guide Service in Moneta, VA.

1. Local Knowledge You Can't Google

Smith Mountain Lake is massive. With 500 miles of shoreline, dozens of creek arms, and depths exceeding 200 feet, knowing where to fish is half the battle. Captain Tommy Moore has spent years building a mental map of this lake — where fish hold in every season, how they react to weather changes, which structures produce consistently, and which ones are overrated.

That kind of knowledge takes decades to build. When you hire a guide, you're renting that entire library of experience for a day. You'll learn more in one guided trip than you would in a dozen trips on your own.

2. Professional Equipment — No Investment Required

Serious striper fishing requires serious gear. Quality rods, reels, line, terminal tackle, electronics, and a properly rigged boat add up fast. When you book with SML Wicked Striper Guide Service, all of that is included:

  • Professional-grade rods and reels spooled with fresh line
  • All terminal tackle and rigging
  • Fresh live bait — gizzard shad and herring
  • State-of-the-art Humminbird fish finders and GPS
  • A safe, well-maintained boat with all required Coast Guard safety equipment

You show up, you fish, you go home. No prep, no cleanup, no gear maintenance.

3. Dramatically Higher Catch Rates

Let's be honest — the main reason to hire a fishing guide is to catch more fish. And that's exactly what happens. Captain Tommy's clients consistently outfish DIY anglers on Smith Mountain Lake because he knows the patterns, has the right bait, and positions the boat with surgical precision.

He's also constantly adapting. If the morning pattern fizzles, he has backup spots and alternative techniques ready. That flexibility is the difference between a great day and a frustrating one.

4. Safety on the Water

Smith Mountain Lake is a big body of water with boat traffic, variable weather, and areas that can be tricky to navigate. Captain Tommy is licensed, insured, and trained in boating safety. He monitors weather conditions constantly and makes smart decisions to keep you safe. For families with children, that peace of mind is invaluable.

5. Learning Experience

A good fishing guide doesn't just catch fish for you — he teaches you. Captain Tommy enjoys sharing his knowledge about striper behavior, bait selection, presentation techniques, and reading electronics. Many of his clients become better anglers after just one trip, and that knowledge pays dividends for years.

6. Stress-Free Fishing

No trailering a boat. No finding a ramp. No rigging tackle at 5 AM. No worrying about whether you have the right bait or if you're in the right spot. A guided trip strips away all the stress and lets you focus on the one thing you came to do: fish.

7. Perfect for Special Occasions

Father-son trips, birthday outings, bachelor parties, corporate team-building — guided fishing trips on Smith Mountain Lake make incredible memories. Captain Tommy has hosted countless special occasions and knows how to make the day meaningful for everyone on board.

Ready to See the Difference?

Whether you're a seasoned angler looking for local expertise or a first-timer who needs everything taken care of, Captain Tommy Moore delivers. Book your guided fishing trip today or call (434) 610-3255. Check our rates — you'll find they're a great value for what you get.

title: Striped Bass Fishing Techniques | How to Catch Stripers
metaDescription: Master striped bass fishing techniques used by pros at Smith Mountain Lake. Learn how to catch stripers with live bait, trolling, jigging & topwater methods.
content:
Striped Bass Fishing Techniques That Actually Work

Want to know how to catch stripers consistently at Smith Mountain Lake? It starts with understanding the proven striped bass fishing techniques that Captain Tommy Moore uses every day on the water. These aren't internet theories — they're battle-tested methods refined over years of guiding on one of Virginia's premier striper fisheries.

Here's your insider's guide to the techniques that put fish in the boat, courtesy of SML Wicked Striper Guide Service in Moneta, VA.

Downlining Live Bait

If there's one technique that defines striper fishing at Smith Mountain Lake, it's downlining. This method involves lowering live bait — typically gizzard shad or herring — on a weighted line to the exact depth where stripers are holding. Captain Tommy uses his Humminbird electronics to locate fish schools and determine their precise depth, then presents bait right in their face.

Key elements of successful downlining:

  • Fresh, lively bait — Sluggish bait catches sluggish results. Captain Tommy nets fresh shad before every trip.
  • Precise depth control — A 3-foot difference can mean the difference between hookups and silence.
  • Proper hook placement — Through the back or nose, depending on current and presentation style.
  • Line management — Keeping lines tangle-free when multiple rods are in the water.

Free-Lining Live Bait

When stripers are feeding aggressively near the surface or in shallow water, free-lining is deadly. This technique uses little to no weight, allowing live bait to swim naturally. It's especially effective during spring when fish are in tributary arms and during early-morning surface feeds in summer.

Captain Tommy often pairs free-lining with downlining, running some rods deep and some near the surface to cover the entire water column.

Trolling

Trolling is an excellent search technique for covering water and locating scattered fish. Using umbrella rigs, diving plugs, or spoons pulled behind the boat at controlled speeds, trolling lets you present baits across a wide area. It's particularly effective in fall when stripers are roaming and in winter when they're following bait schools along main-lake channels.

Captain Tommy uses planer boards to spread his trolling spread wider, covering more water and presenting baits at different distances from the boat to avoid spooking wary fish.

Vertical Jigging

When stripers are stacked tight on deep structure — a submerged hump, rock pile, or channel ledge — vertical jigging can be incredibly effective. Drop a heavy jig (1 to 3 ounces) directly below the boat, let it fall to the fish's level, then work it with sharp upward snaps and controlled falls.

This technique shines in winter when fish are lethargic and holding tight to structure. The erratic action of a jigged bait triggers reaction strikes from fish that might ignore a slow-moving live bait presentation.

Topwater Fishing

Nothing in freshwater fishing compares to the adrenaline rush of a 30-pound striper crushing a topwater plug. Surface fishing at Smith Mountain Lake happens primarily during low-light conditions — dawn, dusk, and overcast days. When stripers herd bait to the surface, the resulting feeding frenzy is visible from hundreds of yards away as water erupts with explosions.

Large walking baits, poppers, and prop baits all produce, but timing and boat positioning are critical. Captain Tommy reads bird activity, surface disturbances, and his electronics to get you in position before the blitz starts.

Matching Technique to Conditions

The real skill isn't knowing these techniques — it's knowing when to use each one. Captain Tommy reads water temperature, clarity, weather, time of year, bait activity, and fish behavior to select the right approach. Some days it's all downlining. Other days, it's a combination of three or four techniques. That adaptability is what separates a guide from a guy with a boat.

Want to learn these techniques firsthand? Book a trip with Captain Tommy Moore and experience what real striper fishing is all about. Call (434) 610-3255 today.

title: Family Fishing Trips Smith Mountain Lake | Kids Welcome
metaDescription: Plan the perfect family fishing trip at Smith Mountain Lake. Kid-friendly fishing charters with Captain Tommy Moore. Safe, fun & unforgettable for all ages.
content:
Family Fishing Trips at Smith Mountain Lake

Looking for the ultimate family fishing trip at Smith Mountain Lake? Captain Tommy Moore and SML Wicked Striper Guide Service specialize in creating safe, fun, and memorable days on the water for families of all ages. Whether your kids have never held a rod or your teenagers are ready to battle a trophy striper, our kids fishing charter trips are designed with families in mind.

Based in Moneta, VA, we're proud to introduce the next generation of anglers to the incredible fishing that Smith Mountain Lake offers — right here in Virginia's beautiful Blue Ridge Mountains.

Why Smith Mountain Lake Is Perfect for Family Fishing

Smith Mountain Lake isn't just a great striper fishery — it's a fantastic family destination. The lake is surrounded by the scenic Blue Ridge Mountains, the water is clean and clear, and the atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming. Unlike saltwater charters where rough seas and long runs can make kids miserable, Smith Mountain Lake is a calm, protected freshwater lake with short runs to fishing spots.

That means:

  • No seasickness worries
  • Short distances to fishing areas — kids aren't waiting an hour to start
  • Calm water conditions on most days
  • Scenic surroundings that everyone enjoys
  • Restroom breaks are easy — the marina is never far away

Captain Tommy's Approach with Families

Captain Tommy has hosted hundreds of families on Smith Mountain Lake, and he knows that a successful family trip looks different from a hardcore fishing trip. His approach with families focuses on three things: safety, fun, and fish-catching.

Safety first. Every passenger gets a Coast Guard-approved life jacket. Captain Tommy maintains his boat to the highest standards and follows all safety protocols. He's attentive to weather conditions and always makes conservative decisions when families with young children are aboard.

Keep it fun. Captain Tommy is patient with young anglers. He helps with casting, baiting hooks, unhooking fish, and taking photos. He keeps the energy positive and celebrates every catch, whether it's a 2-pound bass or a 20-pound striper. If kids get restless, he mixes things up — maybe some casting practice, exploring a cove, or watching an eagle's nest.

Catch fish. Kids want to catch fish. So Captain Tommy targets species and spots that maximize action. That might mean starting with some easy panfish to build confidence before moving to stripers, or it might mean going straight to a hot striper bite if conditions are right. He reads the group and adjusts accordingly.

What Age Is Appropriate?

Captain Tommy has taken kids as young as 4 years old on fishing trips. For very young children (4-6), half-day trips are recommended — their attention span is limited, and a 4-hour trip is the sweet spot. For kids 7 and up, full-day trips work great, especially if they've shown interest in fishing.

Teenagers? They're often the most enthusiastic anglers on the boat. There's something about battling a big striper that connects with teenagers in a way that few other activities can.

Making Memories That Last

Ask any adult angler about their earliest fishing memories, and their eyes light up. Family fishing trips create bonds that last a lifetime. The excitement of a child catching their first striper, the teamwork of netting a big fish, the storytelling at dinner afterward — these are the moments that families cherish for decades.

Captain Tommy captures these moments too, encouraging families to take plenty of photos and even helping pose fish shots with the Smith Mountain Lake scenery as a backdrop.

Everything Is Included

Family trips with SML Wicked Striper Guide Service include all rods, reels, tackle, live bait, and safety equipment. Just bring sunscreen, snacks, drinks, and excitement. Captain Tommy handles everything else.

Ready to create lifelong memories for your family? Book your family fishing trip today or call Captain Tommy at (434) 610-3255. Visit our About page to learn more about what makes our trips special.

title: Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Report | What's Biting SML
metaDescription: Get the latest Smith Mountain Lake fishing report. Find out what's biting at SML, current striper patterns, water temps & hot techniques from Captain Tommy.
content:
Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Report: Current Conditions

Welcome to the latest Smith Mountain Lake fishing report from Captain Tommy Moore and SML Wicked Striper Guide Service. If you're wondering what's biting at SML, you've come to the right place. Captain Tommy is on the water multiple days per week out of Moneta, VA, and he's got the real-time intel you need to plan your trip.

This report covers current conditions, active patterns, and what to expect in the coming weeks on Smith Mountain Lake.

Current Water Conditions

Smith Mountain Lake's water conditions change throughout the year, and staying on top of them is key to consistent fishing. Here's what we're tracking right now:

  • Water Temperature: Varies by season — Captain Tommy monitors surface and subsurface temps daily
  • Water Clarity: Smith Mountain Lake typically runs clear to moderately clear, with some stain after heavy rain events
  • Lake Level: Managed by Appalachian Power, levels fluctuate but generally remain within a predictable range
  • Current/Flow: Generation schedules from Leesville Dam can influence bait and striper positioning

Striper Report

Striped bass remain the main attraction at Smith Mountain Lake, and the fishery continues to deliver. Virginia's Department of Wildlife Resources maintains an active stocking program, and natural reproduction supplements the population. The result is a healthy mix of slot fish, keepers, and legitimate trophies.

Current striper patterns shift with the seasons, but Captain Tommy stays on top of them daily. His recent trips have been producing consistent action using live bait presentations targeted at the depths where fish are holding. When you book a trip, you're getting the most current information possible — not a report from last week, but what's happening right now.

Bait Situation

The health of the baitfish population directly affects striper fishing quality. Smith Mountain Lake supports strong populations of gizzard shad, blueback herring, and alewife — the primary forage species for stripers. Captain Tommy nets fresh bait before each trip, which makes a huge difference in catch rates compared to using day-old or store-bought bait.

Other Species Report

While stripers are our primary target, Smith Mountain Lake also offers excellent fishing for:

  • Largemouth Bass: Healthy population with fish up to 8+ pounds. Good structure fishing along docks, laydowns, and rocky banks.
  • Smallmouth Bass: Found in the clearer, rockier sections of the lake. Hard-fighting fish up to 4-5 pounds.
  • Catfish: Blue catfish are increasingly common in SML. Some real giants have been caught in recent years.
  • Crappie: Excellent crappie fishing, especially in spring around brush piles and standing timber.

What to Expect in the Coming Weeks

As seasons transition, so do the patterns. Captain Tommy watches water temperature trends, bait movements, and weather forecasts to anticipate where fishing will be best in the coming weeks. This forward-looking approach means he's often ahead of the curve, finding fish before other anglers catch on to new patterns.

Booking a trip 1-2 weeks out is ideal — it gives Captain Tommy time to dial in the current bite and plan the best approach for your specific dates.

How to Use This Report

This fishing report gives you a general picture, but conditions on Smith Mountain Lake can change quickly. Rain events, temperature swings, and generation schedules all influence fish behavior. The best way to get a truly current report is to call Captain Tommy directly at (434) 610-3255. He'll give you an honest assessment of current conditions and help you decide the best time to come.

Better yet, let him show you in person. Book your fishing trip today and experience Smith Mountain Lake's incredible striper fishing firsthand.

title: Virginia Fishing License Guide | VA DWR Requirements
metaDescription: Everything you need to know about Virginia fishing licenses for Smith Mountain Lake. VA DWR requirements, costs, exemptions & how to buy your license online.
content:
Virginia Fishing License: What You Need for Smith Mountain Lake

Planning a fishing trip to Smith Mountain Lake? Before you hit the water, you'll need to understand Virginia fishing license requirements. This guide covers everything you need to know about VA DWR (Department of Wildlife Resources) licensing, so you can fish legally and focus on catching stripers with Captain Tommy Moore.

Here's the straightforward breakdown from SML Wicked Striper Guide Service in Moneta, VA.

Do I Need a Fishing License at Smith Mountain Lake?

Yes. Virginia law requires anyone 16 years of age or older to possess a valid fishing license when fishing in the Commonwealth's public waters, including Smith Mountain Lake. This applies to both residents and non-residents, whether you're fishing from a boat, dock, or shoreline.

Types of Virginia Fishing Licenses

The Virginia DWR offers several license types to fit different needs:

  • Resident Freshwater Fishing License: For Virginia residents fishing in freshwater. Available annually or for shorter terms.
  • Non-Resident Freshwater Fishing License: For out-of-state anglers. Available as annual, short-term, or daily licenses.
  • 1-Day Trip License: Perfect for visitors who are only fishing one day — an economical choice for a guided trip.
  • 5-Day Trip License: Great for vacation anglers planning multiple days on the water.
  • Annual License: Best value if you plan to fish Virginia waters multiple times per year.

Who Is Exempt?

Virginia provides fishing license exemptions for:

  • Children under 16 years old
  • Virginia residents 65 years or older (free license — but you still need to register)
  • Active-duty military personnel on leave (with valid military ID)
  • Licensed Virginia landowners fishing on their own property
  • Participants on designated free fishing days (Virginia hosts several each year)

If your kids are under 16, they can fish on Captain Tommy's boat without a license — one less thing to worry about for your family fishing trip.

How to Buy Your Virginia Fishing License

Purchasing a Virginia fishing license is easy and can be done several ways:

  • Online: Visit the Virginia DWR website (gooutdoorsvirginia.com) and purchase your license instantly. Print it or save the digital version on your phone.
  • By Phone: Call the DWR license sales line.
  • In Person: Many sporting goods stores, bait shops, and Walmart locations in the Smith Mountain Lake area sell Virginia fishing licenses.

Captain Tommy recommends buying your license online before your trip. It takes about 5 minutes and saves time on the morning of your charter.

Striped Bass Regulations at Smith Mountain Lake

In addition to your fishing license, you'll need to know the current regulations for striped bass at Smith Mountain Lake. Virginia DWR sets specific rules including:

  • Minimum size limits
  • Daily creel (bag) limits
  • Slot limits (when applicable)
  • Seasonal restrictions

These regulations can change annually, so always check the current Virginia freshwater fishing regulations before your trip. Captain Tommy stays up-to-date on all current regulations and ensures every trip is fully compliant.

Don't Let Licensing Stress You Out

The licensing process is straightforward, and Captain Tommy is happy to answer any questions when you book your trip. He'll remind you to get your license squared away beforehand and can point you to the right resources.

Got your license? Great — now book your Smith Mountain Lake fishing trip with Captain Tommy Moore. Call (434) 610-3255 to reserve your date.

title: Beginner Striper Fishing | Your First Charter Guide
metaDescription: New to striper fishing? This beginner's guide covers everything for your first fishing charter at Smith Mountain Lake. Tips, what to expect & how to prepare.
content:
Beginner Striper Fishing: Your First Charter at Smith Mountain Lake

Never been beginner striper fishing before? You're in for an incredible experience. Booking your first fishing charter at Smith Mountain Lake with Captain Tommy Moore is the perfect way to get started — no experience necessary. SML Wicked Striper Guide Service in Moneta, VA is built for anglers of all skill levels, and beginners are always welcome.

Here's everything you need to know to make your first striper fishing trip a great one.

What Is Striper Fishing?

Striped bass (also called stripers or rockfish) are powerful, hard-fighting freshwater game fish that can grow to 50+ pounds in Smith Mountain Lake. They're the king of the lake — fast, strong, and challenging to catch consistently without the right knowledge and equipment. That's exactly why a guided charter is the best way to start.

Unlike bass fishing where you're casting to visible cover, striper fishing at Smith Mountain Lake is more technical. Fish are often suspended in open water at specific depths, and locating them requires electronics, experience, and quality live bait. Captain Tommy handles all of that — your job is to hold on when a big striper hits.

Do I Need Any Experience?

Absolutely not. Captain Tommy has introduced hundreds of first-time anglers to striper fishing, and he's incredibly patient and thorough. Before lines go in the water, he'll walk you through:

  • How to hold and operate the rods and reels
  • How to detect a bite and when to set the hook
  • Proper fish-fighting technique (letting the rod do the work)
  • How to safely handle fish for photos and release
  • Basic safety and boat etiquette

You don't need to know anything going in. You'll know plenty coming out.

What Does a First Charter Look Like?

Here's a typical timeline for a half-day trip:

  • Pre-trip: Captain Tommy confirms meeting time and location (typically a marina near Moneta, VA)
  • Arrival: Meet at the dock, load up, and get a safety briefing
  • Bait run: Captain Tommy may net fresh shad before heading to fishing spots
  • Fishing: 3-4 hours of active fishing with hands-on instruction
  • Photos & wrap-up: Photos with your catch, tips for next time, back at the dock

Tips for Your First Striper Charter

Captain Tommy wants your first trip to be amazing. Here are his top tips for beginners:

Dress in layers. Mornings on Smith Mountain Lake can be cool, even in summer. You can always peel off a layer, but you can't add what you didn't bring.

Wear non-marking, non-slip shoes. Tennis shoes or deck shoes are perfect. Avoid flip-flops, sandals, or bare feet.

Bring sunscreen and sunglasses. Reflection off the water amplifies UV exposure significantly. Polarized sunglasses also help you see fish activity in the water.

Eat breakfast. Don't show up on an empty stomach. A good breakfast sets you up for an energetic morning on the water.

Ask questions. Captain Tommy loves teaching. Don't hesitate to ask why he's doing something, what the electronics are showing, or how a technique works. The more you ask, the more you learn.

Will I Actually Catch Fish?

While fishing is never guaranteed (they don't call it "catching"), Captain Tommy's success rate with beginners is excellent. His live bait techniques are forgiving for new anglers — when a striper eats a live shad, it's hard to miss. And because he puts you right on the fish, your odds are dramatically better than trying to figure it out alone.

Most first-time clients leave with multiple fish catches, a new appreciation for the sport, and plans for their next trip.

Ready for your first striper fishing adventure? Book your charter with Captain Tommy Moore today or call (434) 610-3255. No experience needed — just enthusiasm.

title: Smith Mountain Lake Best Fishing Spots & Hidden Gems
metaDescription: Discover the best fishing spots on Smith Mountain Lake. Captain Tommy Moore reveals SML hidden gems, top striper areas & where trophy fish hold year-round.
content:
Smith Mountain Lake Best Fishing Spots and Hidden Gems

Every angler wants to know the Smith Mountain Lake best fishing spots, and Captain Tommy Moore knows them better than almost anyone. After years of guiding on SML, he's catalogued an extensive list of productive structure, holding areas, and SML hidden gems that consistently produce striped bass throughout the year.

While a guide never gives away all his secrets, here's a look at the types of spots that make Smith Mountain Lake such an incredible striper fishery — and why fishing them with a local expert makes all the difference.

Main-Lake Humps and Ridges

Smith Mountain Lake has numerous underwater humps and ridges that don't show on surface-level observation but light up like Christmas trees on sonar. These submerged structures rise from deep water and create current breaks and ambush points that stripers love.

The best humps top out in the 20- to 35-foot range with deep water (60+ feet) surrounding them. Baitfish congregate around these structures, and stripers stack up below them, feeding opportunistically. Captain Tommy has GPS waypoints for dozens of these structures and knows which ones produce during specific seasons and conditions.

Creek Arm Mouths

Where creek arms meet the main lake body, there's usually a subtle depth transition and current seam that concentrates bait and predator fish. These junction points are especially productive in spring (when stripers move into tributaries) and fall (when cooling water pushes bait back toward deeper water).

Smith Mountain Lake has numerous creek arms — Blackwater River, Roanoke River channel, and dozens of smaller tributaries. Each one creates a unique feeding opportunity that Captain Tommy knows how to exploit.

Channel Bends and Ledges

The old Roanoke River channel winds through Smith Mountain Lake, and every bend creates a ledge where the bottom drops from shallow to deep. Stripers patrol these ledges like highways, using the depth change for cover and ambush. Winter fishing on these ledges can be exceptional, with fish stacked in tight schools along the steepest breaks.

Bridge Pilings and Man-Made Structure

Smith Mountain Lake has several bridges, and the concrete pilings create artificial reef-like structure that attracts bait and game fish. The shade, current break, and vertical surfaces these pilings provide make them year-round holding spots for stripers of all sizes.

Deep-Water Suspending Areas

Some of the best striper fishing on Smith Mountain Lake happens over nothing — literally. Open-water areas where bait schools suspend over deep channels attract roaming stripers that follow the food. These spots don't look like much on a map, but Captain Tommy's electronics reveal massive schools of bait and the predators lurking below.

These open-water bites require precise boat positioning and depth control — skills that Captain Tommy has refined over years of guiding.

The Hidden Gems

Beyond the well-known spots, Smith Mountain Lake has countless hidden gems that only locals who spend serious time on the water discover. A submerged rock pile here, an old road bed there, a single standing tree in 40 feet of water — these are the spots that don't appear in fishing forums or magazine articles. They're discovered through years of exploring, marking waypoints, and pattern recognition.

Captain Tommy's waypoint library is one of his most valuable assets. Each point represents a location he's caught fish, and over the years that library has grown into a comprehensive map of SML's most productive areas.

Why Spots Alone Aren't Enough

Here's the truth: even if you had every one of Captain Tommy's waypoints, you still wouldn't fish them as effectively as he does. Knowing the spot is only half the equation. You also need to know:

  • What time of year each spot produces best
  • What depth to present bait at each location
  • How weather and water conditions affect each spot
  • Which direction to approach without spooking fish
  • When to move versus when to stay and wait

That's the value of fishing with Captain Tommy Moore. He pairs his spot knowledge with real-time decision-making that maximizes your time on productive water.

Book your trip today and fish Smith Mountain Lake's best spots with the guide who knows them best. Call (434) 610-3255.

title: What to Bring on a Fishing Charter | Packing List
metaDescription: Complete fishing trip packing list for your Smith Mountain Lake charter. What to bring, what to wear & what Captain Tommy Moore provides on every guided trip.
content:
What to Bring on Your Fishing Charter at Smith Mountain Lake

Booked your trip with Captain Tommy Moore? Great — now let's make sure you're prepared. Knowing what to bring on a fishing charter makes the difference between a comfortable, enjoyable day and a miserable one. This fishing trip packing list covers everything you need (and don't need) for your Smith Mountain Lake guided trip with SML Wicked Striper Guide Service.

What Captain Tommy Provides

First, let's cover what you DON'T need to bring because it's already included with your charter:

  • All fishing rods, reels, and tackle
  • Fresh live bait (gizzard shad and herring)
  • Coast Guard-approved life jackets for every passenger
  • Fish-finding electronics and navigation equipment
  • Cooler with ice for keeping your catch

Leave your fishing gear at home. Captain Tommy's equipment is professional-grade, properly maintained, and matched to the type of fishing you'll be doing. His tackle is always rigged and ready — you won't waste precious fishing time tying knots.

Essential Items to Bring

Here's your must-have packing list:

Sun Protection

  • Sunscreen (SPF 30+ minimum, waterproof) — reapply every 2 hours
  • Polarized sunglasses (protects your eyes AND helps you see fish)
  • Hat with a brim (baseball cap or wide-brim hat)
  • Lightweight long-sleeve shirt or sun hoodie (UPF-rated is ideal)

Clothing

  • Dress in layers — mornings on Smith Mountain Lake are often cool, even in summer
  • Rain jacket (lightweight, packable — Virginia weather changes fast)
  • Non-marking, non-slip shoes (tennis shoes, deck shoes, or rubber-soled boots)
  • In cold months: warm layers, waterproof outer shell, gloves, and a beanie

Food & Drinks

  • Water bottles — stay hydrated, especially in summer
  • Snacks (granola bars, sandwiches, fruit, trail mix)
  • Coffee or your preferred morning beverage
  • A small soft cooler for your food and drinks

Other Essentials

  • Valid Virginia fishing license (buy online beforehand — see our licensing guide)
  • Camera or charged smartphone for photos
  • Any personal medications you may need
  • Motion sickness medication if you're prone (though Smith Mountain Lake is calm)

What NOT to Bring

Just as important as what to bring is what to leave behind:

  • Hard-soled shoes or boots with black soles — they mark up the boat deck
  • Glass bottles — breakage hazard on a boat
  • Excessive gear — the boat has limited storage, so pack light
  • Heavy cologne or perfume — fish can detect scents through the water
  • Bananas — yes, it's a real fishing superstition, and Captain Tommy respects the tradition
  • Negative attitudes — fishing is supposed to be fun

Tips by Season

Spring (March-May): Mornings are chilly but afternoons warm up. Layers are critical. Bring a light rain jacket — spring showers are common in the Blue Ridge Mountains.

Summer (June-August): Heat and sun are your main enemies. Prioritize sun protection, hydration, and lightweight, breathable clothing. Bring extra water.

Fall (September-November): Similar to spring — layer up. The transition from warm to cool can happen quickly. Beautiful fall foliage makes for great photos.

Winter (December-February): Dress warm. Insulated, waterproof outer layers, thermal base layers, waterproof gloves, and a warm hat. Hand warmers are a nice luxury. The fishing can be outstanding, but comfort requires preparation.

Pro Tips from Captain Tommy

  • Apply sunscreen BEFORE you get to the dock — it needs 15-20 minutes to absorb
  • Bring a ziplock bag for your phone and wallet
  • Eat a solid breakfast before the trip
  • Charge your phone the night before — you'll want it for photos
  • Arrive 10-15 minutes early to get settled

Ready to go? Book your Smith Mountain Lake fishing charter with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255. Check our pricing page for trip options and rates.

title: Live Bait vs Lures for Stripers | Best Bait for SML
metaDescription: Live bait vs lures for striper fishing at Smith Mountain Lake. Captain Tommy Moore breaks down the best bait options, when to use each & why live bait wins.
content:
Live Bait vs Lures for Stripers at Smith Mountain Lake

The live bait vs lures debate has raged among striper anglers for decades, and at Smith Mountain Lake, both have their place. But when it comes to consistently putting trophy-class striped bass in the boat, Captain Tommy Moore has a clear favorite. Here's his honest breakdown of the best bait for Smith Mountain Lake striper fishing.

Why Live Bait Dominates at Smith Mountain Lake

Captain Tommy uses live bait as his primary presentation, and for good reason — it outproduces lures by a significant margin in most situations at SML. Here's why:

Natural presentation. A live gizzard shad swimming at 30 feet looks, smells, moves, and vibrates exactly like what stripers eat every day. No lure in existence can perfectly replicate all of those sensory triggers simultaneously. Smith Mountain Lake stripers see plenty of artificial baits, especially in high-traffic areas. Live bait doesn't trigger those learned avoidance behaviors.

Scent trail. Live bait emits natural scent and oils that attract stripers from a distance. In Smith Mountain Lake's clear water, visual appeal matters — but scent is what often triggers the final commitment to eat.

Foolproof for all skill levels. A live shad does the work for you. It swims, it struggles, it attracts attention. For beginners on their first charter, live bait fishing is far more productive because the bait does the heavy lifting. There's no retrieve cadence to master, no rod action to perfect.

Captain Tommy's Live Bait Arsenal

The go-to live baits at Smith Mountain Lake include:

  • Gizzard Shad: The bread and butter. These thick-bodied baitfish are the primary forage for SML stripers, and they're hardy on the hook. Captain Tommy nets fresh shad before every trip using a cast net.
  • Blueback Herring: Smaller and more active than gizzard shad, herring are excellent when stripers are keyed on smaller forage. Their frantic swimming action drives stripers wild.
  • Alewife: Another natural forage species in SML. Similar to herring but with subtle differences that matter on certain days.

Fresh bait is non-negotiable. Captain Tommy won't fish with yesterday's bait. The liveliness and scent of freshly caught shad dramatically outperforms tired, listless baitfish.

When Lures Have the Edge

That said, artificial lures absolutely have their place in striper fishing at Smith Mountain Lake. Here are the situations where Captain Tommy reaches for the tackle box instead of the bait well:

Surface feeding frenzies. When stripers are blasting bait on the surface, there's no time to rig live bait — you need to get a topwater plug or jerkbait in the action immediately. Large walking baits, poppers, and swim baits excel during surface blitzes.

Covering water quickly. Trolling artificial lures (umbrella rigs, diving plugs, spoons) lets you search large areas efficiently. This is valuable when fish are scattered and you need to locate the bite before committing to a specific area.

Tight structure fishing. When stripers are tucked against bridge pilings, rock walls, or other snaggy structure, a precisely placed jig or swimbait can access spots where live bait rigs would hang up.

Heavy current situations. During heavy generation from Leesville Dam, strong current can make live bait presentations difficult. Heavy jigs and bucktails that maintain contact with the bottom produce well in current.

Popular Artificial Lures for SML Stripers

  • Umbrella rigs (A-rigs): Simulate a school of baitfish — deadly for trolling
  • Swimbaits (3-6 inch): Paddle-tail soft plastics on jig heads
  • Bucktail jigs (1-3 oz): Classic striper lure for vertical jigging
  • Topwater plugs: Walking baits and poppers for surface feeding
  • Crankbaits: Deep-diving plugs for trolling specific depth zones

The Verdict

For consistent striper catching at Smith Mountain Lake, live bait wins. Captain Tommy estimates that 80% of the fish his clients catch come on live bait. But the best anglers — and the best guides — know how to use both effectively. On Captain Tommy's boat, you'll experience the most productive approach for the day's conditions, whether that's live bait, lures, or a combination.

Want to see what's working right now? Book your trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255. He'll put the right bait in front of the right fish.

title: Night Fishing Smith Mountain Lake | Striper After Dark
metaDescription: Experience night fishing at Smith Mountain Lake for giant stripers. Captain Tommy Moore's guide to after-dark striper fishing tactics, safety & best seasons.
content:
Night Fishing Smith Mountain Lake: Stripers After Dark

Night fishing at Smith Mountain Lake is one of the best-kept secrets among serious striper anglers. While most fishermen are back at the dock grilling their catch, the real trophy hunters know that striper night fishing can produce some of the biggest fish of the year. Captain Tommy Moore of SML Wicked Striper Guide Service in Moneta, VA shares his insights on this exciting after-dark fishery.

Why Stripers Feed at Night

Striped bass are crepuscular and nocturnal predators by nature — meaning they're naturally wired to hunt during low-light conditions and after dark. At Smith Mountain Lake, this behavior is amplified by several factors:

Reduced boat traffic. During summer, Smith Mountain Lake sees heavy recreational boat traffic during the day. Wakes, engine noise, and human activity push stripers deeper and make them more cautious. At night, the lake goes quiet, and fish move shallower and feed more aggressively.

Baitfish vulnerability. Gizzard shad and herring are less aware of predators in darkness. They drift into shallower water and congregate around lights, structure, and current areas where stripers can ambush them easily.

Water temperature relief. In summer, surface temperatures at SML can exceed 85 degrees. Stripers are cold-water fish that prefer temperatures in the 60s. At night, the upper water column cools slightly, and fish that were pinned in the thermocline during the day become more active and range further.

Best Seasons for Night Fishing SML

Night fishing at Smith Mountain Lake produces year-round, but certain seasons stand out:

Late Spring (May-June): Post-spawn stripers are ravenous and actively feeding at night. The combination of warming water, long days, and hungry fish creates outstanding after-dark action.

Summer (July-August): This is prime time for night fishing. Daytime heat pushes fish deep, but they come alive after sunset. Some of Captain Tommy's biggest fish each year come during summer night trips.

Early Fall (September-October): As days shorten and bait becomes more concentrated, night fishing remains productive. Cooling water makes fish aggressive, and the transition period produces both numbers and size.

Night Fishing Techniques

Fishing after dark requires adjustments to daytime tactics:

  • Lighted areas: Dock lights, bridge lights, and underwater fishing lights attract baitfish, which in turn attract stripers. Fishing near these light sources can be incredibly productive.
  • Slow presentations: Fish are hunting by lateral line (vibration) and scent more than sight. Slow-moving live bait or subtle lure presentations outperform fast, reaction-style techniques.
  • Shallow structure: Stripers move shallower at night than they hold during the day. Points, humps, and flats that top out in 10-20 feet become productive nighttime spots.
  • Sound discipline: The lake is quiet at night, and fish are hyper-aware of noise. Minimize banging, talking, and unnecessary movement on the boat.

Safety Considerations

Night fishing adds a layer of complexity that requires experience and preparation. Captain Tommy takes night fishing safety seriously:

  • All required navigation lights are in perfect working order
  • Every passenger wears a life jacket
  • GPS and depth-finding electronics guide navigation
  • Flashlights and headlamps with red-light modes are available
  • Familiarity with the lake eliminates navigation guesswork
  • Weather is monitored closely — night trips are cancelled if conditions are questionable

The Night Fishing Experience

There's something magical about being on Smith Mountain Lake at night. The Blue Ridge Mountains form a dark silhouette against the stars. The water is glass-calm. Occasional splashes from feeding fish break the silence. And then your rod loads up with the unmistakable weight of a big striper, and the fight begins in total darkness — guided only by the sound of drag screaming and the bend of the rod.

It's a sensory experience unlike any daytime fishing trip, and many of Captain Tommy's clients who try it once become regular night-fishing customers.

Book a Night Fishing Trip

Night fishing trips at Smith Mountain Lake are available seasonally. Contact Captain Tommy Moore to discuss availability and conditions. Book your trip online or call (434) 610-3255 to experience striper fishing after dark.

content:
Fishing Charter Rates at Smith Mountain Lake

Captain Tommy Moore offers competitive SML guide prices that deliver incredible value — because every trip includes professional equipment, fresh live bait, and decades of Smith Mountain Lake expertise.

Here's what you need to know about booking a guided striper fishing trip out of Moneta, VA.

What's Included in Every Trip

Before we talk numbers, understand what you're getting. Every guided fishing trip with Captain Tommy Moore includes:

  • All fishing equipment: Professional-grade rods, reels, line, hooks, weights, and terminal tackle

Half-day trips typically depart early morning or mid-morning and provide plenty of time to catch fish and learn the basics. This is our most popular option for beginners and families.

Full-Day Trip (7-8 hours): The ultimate Smith Mountain Lake striper experience. Full-day trips give Captain Tommy time to explore multiple spots, try different techniques, and wait out slow periods. If you're serious about catching trophy fish or maximizing your day on the water, the full-day trip is the way to go.

For current rates and seasonal pricing, visit our pricing page or call Captain Tommy directly at (434) 610-3255.

How SML Wicked Striper Compares

What's included? Some charters charge extra for bait, tackle, or fuel. With SML Wicked Striper, everything is included in one price. No surprise charges at the dock.

Boat and equipment quality. Captain Tommy's boat is clean, safe, well-maintained, and equipped with top-tier electronics. His rods and reels are professional quality — not bargain-bin combos.

Experience level. You're fishing with a captain who has dedicated years to mastering Smith Mountain Lake specifically. That hyper-local expertise translates directly into more fish caught.

Client reviews. Our repeat booking rate speaks volumes. Many clients come back multiple times per year because they know the value is there.

Booking and Payment

Booking your trip is simple:

Group Trips and Special Occasions

Captain Tommy accommodates groups for corporate outings, bachelor parties, birthday celebrations, and other special events. Contact him directly to discuss group rates and multi-boat options for larger parties.

Is a Guided Trip Worth It?

Consider the alternative: renting a boat, buying tackle, purchasing live bait, fueling up, and spending the day trying to find fish without local knowledge or professional electronics. Between rental costs, fuel, bait, and tackle, a DIY trip often approaches the cost of a guided trip — without the expertise, equipment quality, or catch rate.

When you factor in what you get with SML Wicked Striper Guide Service, guided trips aren't just competitive — they're the better deal.

Book your trip with Captain Tommy Moore today and experience the best value in Smith Mountain Lake fishing charters.

title: Best Fishing Spots Smith Mountain Lake | Where to Fish
metaDescription: Find the best fishing spots on Smith Mountain Lake. Local guide Captain Tommy Moore shares where to fish SML for stripers, bass, crappie & catfish year-round.
content:
Best Fishing Spots on Smith Mountain Lake

With over 20,000 acres of water and 500 miles of shoreline, figuring out the best fishing spots on Smith Mountain Lake can feel overwhelming. Whether you're a visiting angler trying to plan a trip or a local looking to expand your repertoire, knowing where to fish SML is the single biggest factor in your success. Captain Tommy Moore of SML Wicked Striper Guide Service has spent years mapping the most productive areas of this incredible Virginia fishery.

Understanding Smith Mountain Lake's Layout

Before diving into specific areas, it helps to understand the lake's geography. Smith Mountain Lake is a reservoir formed by Smith Mountain Dam on the Roanoke River. The lake has two main arms — the Roanoke River arm and the Blackwater River arm — that converge near the dam. This Y-shape creates diverse habitat ranging from shallow, creek-like environments in the upper reaches to deep, open-water basins near the dam.

This variety is what makes SML such a productive fishery. Different areas of the lake fish differently depending on the season, and knowing which sections to target at which times is Captain Tommy's specialty.

Upper Roanoke River Arm

The upper Roanoke arm is shallower and more riverine, with visible current during generation periods. This area excels in:

  • Spring: Stripers push upriver during pre-spawn, creating a concentration effect that makes them easier to locate
  • Year-round: Excellent largemouth bass habitat along laydowns, docks, and rocky banks
  • Fall: Bait concentrates in the narrower water, attracting all predator species

Upper Blackwater River Arm

The Blackwater arm offers similar characteristics to the Roanoke arm but with its own unique structure and patterns. Crappie fishing is particularly strong in this section, with numerous brush piles and standing timber that hold fish throughout the year.

Mid-Lake Region

Where the arms begin to widen and deepen, you'll find the mid-lake region — arguably the most versatile section of Smith Mountain Lake. This area features:

  • Numerous underwater humps and ridges that hold stripers year-round
  • Creek arm mouths that create bait-concentrating current seams
  • A mix of deep and shallow water within short distances
  • Less fishing pressure than the easily-accessible upper sections

Captain Tommy spends a significant amount of his fishing time in the mid-lake region because it offers the most consistent striper action across all seasons.

Main Lake Basin (Near the Dam)

The deepest and widest section of Smith Mountain Lake sits near Smith Mountain Dam. Depths exceed 200 feet in some areas, and the open-water characteristics create unique fishing opportunities:

  • Summer: Deep thermocline fishing for suspended stripers in the 30- to 50-foot range
  • Winter: Schools of stripers stack in deep channels and along steep bluff walls
  • Year-round: Trolling the main lake basin covers water efficiently and contacts roaming fish

Points and Peninsulas

Smith Mountain Lake's 500 miles of shoreline create countless points and peninsulas that extend into deeper water. These are ambush points for stripers — places where fish can sit in deep water but quickly move shallow to intercept bait. The best points have a gradual taper with rocks or hard bottom, access to deep water (30+ feet) nearby, and are adjacent to a creek channel or river bend.

Marinas and Dock Areas

While not traditional "fishing spots," marina areas and dock-lined coves hold surprising numbers of fish. Dock lights attract bait at night, and the shade provided by covered slips holds fish during sunny days. These areas are especially productive for largemouth bass and crappie, with occasional stripers cruising through to ambush bait.

Why a Local Guide Matters

The truth is, even detailed spot descriptions can only take you so far. Smith Mountain Lake's fishing changes daily based on weather, water conditions, bait movements, and generation schedules. What's productive on Monday might be dead by Thursday. Captain Tommy is on the water multiple days per week, constantly updating his pattern knowledge and adjusting his approach.

That real-time intelligence is something no fishing report, forum post, or blog article can provide. It's the main reason hiring a guide at Smith Mountain Lake dramatically increases your success.

Ready to fish the best spots on Smith Mountain Lake with the guide who knows them? Book your trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255.

title: Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Vacation | Plan Your Trip
metaDescription: Plan the perfect Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation. Lodging, dining, activities & guided fishing trips in Virginia's Blue Ridge Mountains with Captain Tommy.
content:
Plan Your Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Vacation

A Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation combines world-class striper fishing with the natural beauty of Virginia's Blue Ridge Mountains. Whether you're planning a fishing trip to Virginia for the first time or returning to one of the East Coast's finest freshwater destinations, this guide covers everything you need to know — from booking your guided trip with Captain Tommy Moore to finding the best lodging, dining, and activities in the area.

Getting to Smith Mountain Lake

Smith Mountain Lake is located in Bedford and Franklin Counties in southwestern Virginia, near the town of Moneta, VA 24121. The lake sits about:

  • 30 minutes southeast of Roanoke, VA (nearest major city)
  • 60 minutes south of Lexington, VA
  • 3 hours southwest of Richmond, VA
  • 4 hours southwest of Washington, D.C.
  • 4.5 hours west of Virginia Beach

The closest commercial airport is Roanoke-Blacksburg Regional Airport (ROA), with connections through major hubs. From there, it's an easy 30-minute drive to the lake.

Lodging Options

Smith Mountain Lake offers accommodations for every budget and style:

Lakefront Vacation Rentals: The most popular option for fishing vacations. Hundreds of lakefront homes and cabins are available through rental agencies and platforms like VRBO and Airbnb. Many include private docks where Captain Tommy can pick you up directly — no marina trip required.

Resorts and Hotels: The Mariners Landing resort and other hotels around the lake provide comfortable stays with lake access, pools, restaurants, and other amenities. Ideal for families who want structured activities beyond fishing.

Campgrounds: Smith Mountain Lake State Park offers camping with lake access, hiking trails, and a swimming beach. Several private campgrounds in the area offer RV hookups and tent sites.

Bed and Breakfasts: Charming B&Bs dot the surrounding countryside, offering a more intimate Virginia experience with home-cooked breakfasts that fuel a morning on the water.

Building Your Fishing Itinerary

How many days should you fish? Here's Captain Tommy's recommendation:

Weekend trip (2-3 days): Book one full-day guided trip and spend the other day exploring the lake on your own, relaxing at your rental, or visiting local attractions. One guided day gives you enough time for a quality fishing experience.

Week-long vacation (5-7 days): Book 2-3 guided fishing days spaced throughout the week. This gives you fishing days mixed with relaxation and activity days. Captain Tommy can target different species or techniques each day to keep things fresh.

Dedicated fishing trip (3-5 days): For serious anglers, 3-5 consecutive guided days allows you to really dial in the patterns, learn the lake, and maximize your chances at trophy fish.

Beyond Fishing: Things to Do

Smith Mountain Lake and the surrounding Blue Ridge region offer plenty for non-fishing days:

  • Smith Mountain Lake State Park: Hiking trails, swimming beach, kayak and paddleboard rentals
  • Booker T. Washington National Monument: Historic birthplace site just minutes from the lake
  • Blue Ridge Parkway: America's favorite scenic drive, with overlooks, hiking trails, and Peaks of Otter Lodge nearby
  • Wineries and breweries: Several local vineyards and craft breweries offer tastings with mountain views
  • Water sports: Swimming, kayaking, paddleboarding, tubing, and wakeboarding on the lake
  • Golf: Multiple courses in the area with Blue Ridge Mountain scenery

Dining Around Smith Mountain Lake

The SML area has a growing restaurant scene with options ranging from casual lakeside grills to upscale dining. Fresh seafood, Virginia barbecue, farm-to-table restaurants, and classic American fare are all within easy reach. Many lakeside restaurants are accessible by boat, making lunch stops during your non-guided days convenient and fun.

Best Time to Visit

Smith Mountain Lake is a four-season destination, but each season offers a different experience:

  • Spring (April-May): Mild weather, dogwood blooms, excellent fishing, fewer crowds
  • Summer (June-August): Peak season — warm weather, full lake activities, vibrant community events
  • Fall (September-November): Stunning Blue Ridge foliage, great fishing, comfortable temperatures, reduced crowds
  • Winter (December-February): Quiet and serene, excellent striper fishing, best lodging rates

For the best combination of fishing quality and overall vacation experience, Captain Tommy recommends late April through early June, or September through October.

Start Planning Your Trip

The first step in planning your Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation is securing your guided trip dates. Captain Tommy's calendar fills up, especially during peak season, so booking early is recommended.

Book your guided fishing trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255 to discuss dates, trip options, and local recommendations. He's happy to help you plan the perfect Virginia fishing getaway. Check our pricing page for current rates.

title: Trophy Striper Tips | Catch Big Striped Bass at SML
metaDescription: Pro tips for catching trophy striped bass at Smith Mountain Lake. Captain Tommy Moore shares techniques, timing & strategies for landing 30-50 pound stripers.
content:
Trophy Striper Tips for Smith Mountain Lake

Smith Mountain Lake produces legitimate trophy striped bass every year — fish in the 30-, 40-, and even 50-pound class that test your tackle, your endurance, and your nerve. If catching big striped bass at SML is on your bucket list, Captain Tommy Moore of SML Wicked Striper Guide Service has the playbook to make it happen.

Here are Captain Tommy's hard-earned tips for targeting the biggest stripers in Smith Mountain Lake, refined over years of guiding from Moneta, VA.

Think Big Bait, Big Fish

The single most effective way to target trophy stripers is to upsize your bait. While a 4-inch shad will catch plenty of average fish, the true giants at Smith Mountain Lake want a meal — not a snack. Captain Tommy targets oversized gizzard shad (8-12 inches) when trophy hunting. These big baits filter out smaller fish and trigger strikes from the lake's apex predators.

Big bait requires heavier tackle to present properly. Stout rods, strong reels, and premium line are essential — all provided on Captain Tommy's boat. This isn't light-tackle finesse fishing; it's heavy-duty striper warfare.

Target the Right Time Windows

While trophy stripers are caught year-round, certain periods dramatically increase your odds:

  • Late winter (January-February): Pre-spawn females are at their heaviest, packed with eggs and stored energy. Cold water concentrates them into tight, locatable schools.
  • Early spring (March-April): The pre-spawn feed-up produces aggressive fish that are actively hunting. Size and numbers can both be excellent.
  • Late fall (November): Post-turnover feeding binges put big fish in a vulnerable, aggressive state. The coolest water of fall triggers trophy-class activity.

Captain Tommy keeps detailed records of his biggest fish each year, and these three windows consistently produce the largest stripers.

Go Deep or Go Home

Trophy stripers at Smith Mountain Lake spend the majority of their time deep — deeper than most recreational anglers fish. While average fish might be found at 25-35 feet, the true giants often hold at 40, 50, or even 60+ feet, especially during summer and winter.

Reaching these fish requires precise depth control, heavy weights, and the patience to keep bait in the zone long enough for a big fish to commit. Captain Tommy's electronics show him exactly where the big marks are sitting, and his presentation puts bait right at their level.

Fish the Full Moon

Experienced striper guides know that the full moon period (3 days before through 3 days after) often produces the best trophy fishing. The theory is that increased nighttime light triggers more aggressive nighttime feeding, which carries over into the early morning hours. Whether it's science or superstition, Captain Tommy's catch logs show a clear pattern of bigger fish around the full moon.

Patience Is the Key Ingredient

Trophy hunting is fundamentally different from catch-as-many-as-possible fishing. Some of the best trophy trips involve long periods of waiting between bites, because you're using oversized bait and targeting specific structure that holds the largest fish. A great trophy day might be 2-3 bites — but each one is a potential personal best.

This is where a guide's value becomes obvious. Captain Tommy knows when to stay patient on a spot and when to move. He reads his electronics constantly, watching for the big arches that indicate trophy-class fish. That real-time information keeps you in the right position, even when the waiting tests your resolve.

Proper Fish Handling

When you do hook a trophy striper at Smith Mountain Lake, fighting it properly is critical — both for landing the fish and for its survival if you practice catch and release. Captain Tommy coaches you through every fight:

  • Keep the rod tip up and let the rod absorb the fish's runs
  • Use steady pressure — don't try to muscle a 40-pound fish
  • Pump and reel on the fish's downward head shakes
  • Let the fish run when it needs to — fighting the drag only creates break-offs
  • Support the fish horizontally for photos — never hold a big striper vertically by the jaw

Smith Mountain Lake's Trophy Potential

Virginia's stocking program, combined with abundant forage and deep, well-oxygenated water, gives Smith Mountain Lake legitimate trophy potential that rivals any inland striper fishery in the country. The lake has produced fish over 40 pounds consistently, and the potential for a 50-pounder is real every time you go out.

The Blue Ridge Mountain setting makes these catches even more special — battling a trophy striper with mountain scenery as your backdrop is a uniquely Virginia experience.

Ready to chase your personal best striper? Book a trophy trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255. Tell him you want to go big — he'll plan the trip accordingly.
